commit.message:
[syscall] B: Fix readlink buffer bugs

safe::fs::readlink() had two bugs:

1. Used Vec::with_capacity(PATH_MAX) which creates a zero-length
   buffer, causing bufsiz=0 in the IPC request to linuxd. This
   corrupted nanvixd IPC state and caused subsequent VM tests to hang.
   buffer.

2. Used FileSystemPath::try_from_bytes() which expects NUL-terminated
   input (CString::from_vec_with_nul), but POSIX readlink() does not
   NUL-terminate its output. Fixed by converting via core::str::from_utf8
   and FileSystemPath::new().

Fix circuit-sim for Windows: text rawfile + encoding

- parse_rawfile.py: Support both binary and text (Values:) rawfile
  formats. Windows ngspice outputs text format even with -r flag.
- run_sim.py: Add encoding='utf-8' to all NamedTemporaryFile and
  Path.read_text() calls. Fixes UnicodeEncodeError on Windows
  where the default encoding is cp1252.

rc_sweep) pass end-to-end on Windows with ngspice 45.2.

[ci] E: Restrict image sizes to 128 MB

build and release-archive matrices. Only the 128 MB image is now
built and published.

  that are no longer needed.
- release-archive: narrow memory-size matrix from

The publish-release action discovers artifacts via glob pattern,

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: show default parameter values concisely in signature help (#1512)

* fix: show default parameter values concisely in signature help (#1310)

When a C#-style optional parameter is decorated with
[<Optional; DefaultParameterValue(value)>], signature help previously
showed the full verbose attribute syntax, e.g.:

  [<System.Runtime.InteropServices.Optional;
    System.Runtime.InteropServices.DefaultParameterValue("")>] args: string

Now the attribute block is stripped and the default value is shown inline:

  args: string = ""

If no DefaultParameterValue is present (only [<Optional>]), the attribute
is silently removed leaving just the clean name: type display.

Implementation: add TipFormatter.cleanParameterDisplay which uses a regex
to detect [<...>] blocks, extract any DefaultParameterValue argument, and
reconstruct the parameter string. Use it in TextDocumentSignatureHelp
parameter documentation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

* test: add unit tests for TipFormatter.cleanParameterDisplay

Cover the following scenarios:
- Full verbose C# interop attributes (Optional + DefaultParameterValue) with string default
- Short-form Optional+DefaultParameterValue with string default
- Numeric default value
- Boolean default value
- Optional-only (no DefaultParameterValue) — attribute stripped, no suffix
- Plain parameter with no attribute — returned unchanged
- Multiple tagged-text segments concatenated before processing

All 7 new tests pass alongside the 8 pre-existing TipFormatter tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Only Ontology correctly remains as govern (metadata governance).

commit.message:
Exclude gitignored files from Lambda layer and source code bundles (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: exclude gitignored files from lambda layer and code bundles

- Add gitignore_exclude_patterns() utility to bundler.py that reads the
  root .gitignore and returns all non-comment, non-blank patterns
- DepsBundler.__init__ now stores patterns; try_bundle passes them to
  shutil.copytree(ignore=...) so __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv/, etc. are
  excluded when copying workspace packages into the layer
- hello_stack.py passes exclude=gitignore_exclude_patterns() with
  ignore_mode=IgnoreMode.GIT to Code.from_asset for the lambda handler,
  keeping gitignored artefacts out of the deployment zip
- Add 5 new tests covering the new function and copytree ignore behaviour

Co-authored-by: klemjul <60812459+klemjul@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/klemjul/python-aws-monorepo-boilerplate/sessions/bbd1571f-8fa5-4142-b027-9555f94766b0

* fix: apply ignore callback at top level of workspace src copy

Replace the manual entry-by-entry loop with a single shutil.copytree call
on src_dir directly, so the ignore callback (built from .gitignore patterns)
is applied at every level including the top level of src/. The old loop
only passed ignore= when recursing into sub-directories, meaning top-level
__pycache__/, *.pyc, and similar entries would still be copied into the layer.
